symptom_description: Numerous crimson broad zones blanketed by heavy glossy, pale flakes across the head.

caption: The irregular erythematous plaques and scales seen in this facial photo are typical symptoms of psoriasis. Psoriasis is a chronic, non-infectious autoimmune skin disease that commonly affects the scalp, trunk, and extensor surfaces of the limbs. The exact cause is not clear, but it is believed to be related to factors such as genetics and immune system abnormalities. Treatment options include topical medications, phototherapy, and systemic medications.
symptom_description: Uneven crimson broad zones and flakes across the visage.

caption: The photo shows infiltrative erythema, papules, and extensive coalescence on the upper arm and shoulder. With the clinical symptoms and signs, the diagnosis of nodular disease is confirmed.
symptom_description: Spreading crimson appearance, bumps, and massive merging across the limb.

caption: This is a photo of the face showing multiple follicular red papules. Based on the clinical presentation and medical history, the diagnosis of acne vulgaris is appropriate. Acne vulgaris is a common chronic skin condition that typically occurs during adolescence, characterized by inflammatory papules around hair follicles and scarring after papule rupture.
symptom_description: Numerous crimson bumps following pore patterns across the visage.

caption: This is a photo showing multiple irregular red patches with fine scales on the face and neck, typical of lupus erythematosus. Lupus erythematosus is a chronic autoimmune disease that commonly affects the skin and multiple organs in the body, presenting with symptoms such as rash, arthritis, and kidney involvement. Treatment typically includes anti-inflammatory medications, immunosuppressants, and corticosteroids. Patients require regular follow-up and monitoring to control the condition.
symptom_description: Numerous uneven crimson areas covered by delicate flakes across the visage and throat.

caption: This photo shows multiple papules and plaques on the upper arm of the patient, consistent with the diagnosis of lichen planus. Lichen planus is a common chronic skin disease characterized by red, raised patches or papules on the skin, often found on the limbs. Treatment typically involves topical medications and avoiding triggering factors. Regular follow-up visits are necessary to monitor the progression of the condition and adjust the treatment plan.
symptom_description: Numerous bumps and broad zones across the appendage.

caption: This photo shows multiple round or irregular depigmented patches on both lower limbs, which are typical manifestations of psoriasis. Psoriasis is a chronic, relapsing skin disease characterized by skin proliferation, scaling, and inflammation. Diagnosis can be confirmed through skin manifestations, medical history, and necessary laboratory tests.
symptom_description: Numerous moon-shaped or uneven pale areas across the legs.

caption: This photo shows diffuse plaque psoriasis on the buttocks and lower limbs of the patient, covered with thick scales. This is a typical manifestation of psoriasis. Psoriasis is a chronic recurrent skin disease commonly found on the body joints, presenting as red patches with scales. The etiology of psoriasis is complex and involves various factors such as genetics, immunity, and environment.
symptom_description: Widespread broad zones blanketed by heavy flakes across the rear and legs.

caption: The diagnosis is lichen planus, characterized by multiple flat-topped, grayish-white papules with a linear distribution on the skin. Lichen planus is a common skin condition that is not contagious but can cause itching or discomfort. Diagnosis is usually based on the characteristic skin lesions and clinical presentation. Treatment options include topical corticosteroids, other anti-inflammatory medications, and in severe cases, oral medications or other therapies may be recommended. Seeking timely medical attention and following medical advice are key to managing lichen planus.
symptom_description: Numerous level-peak, ash-colored bumps aligned sequentially.

caption: This is an image of a facial skin lesion showing wax-like, translucent nodules with raised, rolled edges and central ulceration, consistent with a diagnosis of basal cell carcinoma. Basal cell carcinoma is one of the most common types of skin cancer, often appearing on the head and neck, and can be treated with surgical excision or other treatment modalities.
symptom_description: Shiny, partially clear lumps possessing elevated, curled borders and a middle open sore.

caption: The lesion presents as nodules with waxy, semi-transparent appearance, raised and curled edges, central ulceration, consistent with the clinical features of basal cell carcinoma.
symptom_description: Lumps possessing a shiny, partially clear look, elevated and twisting borders, and a middle open sore.

caption: Flat lichen is a chronic, recurrent skin disease characterized by purple-red polygonal plaques with well-defined borders and a waxy sheen on the surface. Skin lesions are often isolated and can significantly affect the patient's quality of life. Treatment mainly involves a comprehensive approach including topical medications, phototherapy, and oral medications.
symptom_description: Plum-crimson multi-sided broad zones possessing distinct boundaries and a glossy top.

caption: This is an image showing diffuse dark red papules on the face, with subsequent pigmentation loss after healing, consistent with the diagnosis of lupus erythematosus. Lupus erythematosus is a chronic autoimmune disease that often affects the skin and multiple organ systems, presenting with a variety of clinical manifestations. Diagnosis relies on clinical presentation and laboratory tests, and treatment involves the use of steroids and immunosuppressants for control.
symptom_description: Widespread deep crimson bumps across the visage, followed by a lack of color upon recovery.

caption: Flat warts is a common skin condition characterized by flat papules or nodules on the skin, which may vary in color and have clear borders. This condition is usually chronic and can occur in different parts of the body, often accompanied by mild itching. Treatment for flat warts may include topical medications, phototherapy, and oral medications, with the specific treatment plan depending on the severity of the condition.
symptom_description: Level bumps or lumps possessing differing shades and distinct boundaries.

caption: This is a common chronic autoimmune skin disease characterized by red patches covered with thick silvery-white scales on the skin surface, commonly seen on the scalp, trunk, limbs, etc. The pathogenesis of psoriasis is related to various factors such as genetics, immunity, and environment. The diagnosis can be made clinically based on the characteristic changes in the affected skin.
symptom_description: Crimson areas blanketed by heavy glossy, pale flakes across the top, frequently spotted near the head and extremities.

caption: This is a picture of skin lesions on the upper limb, showing multiple flat-topped polygonal purplish-red papules with shiny surfaces and clear borders, consistent with the clinical features of lichen planus. Lichen planus is a chronic inflammatory skin disease characterized by purplish-red flat-topped papules or plaques with smooth surfaces or fine scales, commonly found on the extensor surfaces of the limbs, trunk, and face. The diagnosis of lichen planus should be based on the patient's clinical symptoms, medical history, and characteristics of the skin lesions, and a skin biopsy may be necessary for confirmation.
symptom_description: Numerous level-peak multi-sided plum-crimson bumps possessing glossy peaks and distinct boundaries.

caption: Basal cell carcinoma is diagnosed based on the observation of brownish patches, central depression, irregular shape, unclear borders, and dilated blood vessels around the lesion. Basal cell carcinoma is a common type of skin cancer that typically originates from the basal cells beneath the epidermis, presenting with features similar to those described above. This type of cancer usually grows slowly but may invade surrounding tissues if left untreated. It is advisable to seek medical attention promptly for further evaluation and treatment.
symptom_description: Coffee-colored zones possessing a sunken middle, uneven form, blurred boundaries, and widened veins.

caption: This is a photo showing skin lesions on the face. Swelling and redness at the tip of the nose, as well as thinning of the lips, are evident. Based on the symptoms and clinical features, a preliminary diagnosis of scleroderma is made.
symptom_description: Puffy crimson appearance near the smelling organ's peak, paired with a narrowing mouth area.

caption: The patient presents with palm-sized brown patches on the abdomen, along with pigmentation and hypopigmented patches in a reticulated pattern on the inner side of the right thigh. The anterior side of the right thigh appears tense and shiny. These symptoms are indicative of scleroderma, and further investigations and treatment are recommended.
symptom_description: Hand-sized coffee-colored zones across the midsection, paired with darkened and pale spots in a net-like arrangement near the leg. The frontal appendage feels taut and glossy.

caption: On the skin surface, pinpoint-sized follicular white papules with homomorphic reaction can be observed, which typically suggest a diagnosis of lichen planus or lichen planus.
symptom_description: Needle-sized pale bumps following pores, possessing identical responses along scratched lines.

caption: Based on the description of the lesion, the patient has a circular red patch with central regression and raised nodules at the edges under the right outer canthus. The differential diagnoses include sarcoidosis and granuloma annulare. Sarcoidosis is a chronic multi-system disease that can cause tissue proliferation and nodules, commonly affecting the eyes, skin, and other areas. Granuloma annulare, on the other hand, is an inflammatory skin condition characterized by localized redness, swelling, and raised edges. To confirm the diagnosis, it is recommended for the patient to seek medical attention promptly for further evaluation and diagnosis.
symptom_description: A round crimson zone possessing a fading middle and elevated lumps near the boundaries, situated below the exterior visual corner.
